description: XRISM Resolve total observed FWHM at 3.55 keV from the Gaussian forward model. Instrument response sigma_inst = 5.0 eV / 2.355 ~ 2.12 eV (Ishisaki et al. 2022, Resolve calibration); kinematic Doppler sigma_kin = E_obs * (v_turb / c) with E_obs ~ 3547.5 eV (post grav-redshift) and v_turb ~ 300 km/s gives sigma_kin ~ 3.55 eV (this is a Gaussian sigma, not a FWHM). Quadrature sum sigma_total = sqrt(sigma_inst^2 + sigma_kin^2) ~ 4.14 eV; converting back to FWHM via 2.355 * sigma_total yields predicted total observed FWHM ~9.75 eV. Intrinsic Lorentzian width is taken <<1 eV (lattice-locked Mossbauer emission, V3 Ch11) and contributes negligibly to the quadrature sum. The deconvolved intrinsic line-width residual W_int observable is embedded in the full Protocol C preregistration: terminal no-line non-detection at the Bulbul-level empirical-derived floor Gamma <= 2.0e-28 s^-1 in >=94 Ms equivalent stacked exposure, W_int > 20 eV after instrument and registered kinematic/turbulence deconvolution, or morphology failing the stress-gated rho_above_sigma_crit^2 template by Delta chi^2 >= 9 versus both smooth rho and smooth rho^2 inside the sigma_crit = 0.53 keV cm^-3 frozen aperture falsifies. The 26 Ms / Gamma <= 3.8e-28 s^-1 stack remains a morphology-linewidth milestone; the background-limited Gamma <= 1.2e-30 s^-1 calculation is theoretical, not operative. Engine target here is the total convolved FWHM as predicted by the forward model.
disposition_text: XRISM Resolve total observed FWHM at 3.55 keV from the Gaussian forward model. Instrument response sigma_inst = 5.0 eV / 2.355 ~ 2.12 eV (Ishisaki et al. 2022, Resolve calibration); kinematic Doppler sigma_kin = E_obs * (v_turb / c) with E_obs ~ 3547.5 eV (post grav-redshift) and v_turb ~ 300 km/s gives sigma_kin ~ 3.55 eV (this is a Gaussian sigma, not a FWHM). Quadrature sum sigma_total = sqrt(sigma_inst^2 + sigma_kin^2) ~ 4.14 eV; converting back to FWHM via 2.355 * sigma_total yields predicted total observed FWHM ~9.75 eV. Intrinsic Lorentzian width is taken <<1 eV (lattice-locked Mossbauer emission, V3 Ch11) and contributes negligibly to the quadrature sum. The deconvolved intrinsic line-width residual W_int observable is embedded in the full Protocol C preregistration: terminal no-line non-detection at the Bulbul-level empirical-derived floor Gamma <= 2.0e-28 s^-1 in >=94 Ms equivalent stacked exposure, W_int > 20 eV after instrument and registered kinematic/turbulence deconvolution, or morphology failing the stress-gated rho_above_sigma_crit^2 template by Delta chi^2 >= 9 versus both smooth rho and smooth rho^2 inside the sigma_crit = 0.53 keV cm^-3 frozen aperture falsifies. The 26 Ms / Gamma <= 3.8e-28 s^-1 stack remains a morphology-linewidth milestone; the background-limited Gamma <= 1.2e-30 s^-1 calculation is theoretical, not operative. Engine target here is the total convolved FWHM as predicted by the forward model.

description: Muon mass matches m_e * phi^11 (1 + 5*alpha + phi^8 * alpha^2)
disposition_text: Muon mass matches m_e * phi^11 (1 + 5*alpha + phi^8 * alpha^2)
derivation_note: Tree plus single 1-loop GCT correction exponent phi^8 = phi^11 * phi^-3, with phi^-3 the electroweak mixing factor on the bare muon scaling phi^11 (V3 Ch08 §8.2.3; engine: verify_independent/verify_muon_mass.py uses PHI_POWER_2 = 8). Target value is the PDG anchor M_MU_OBS = 105.6584 MeV; tolerance set at the App R / FM headline of ~21 ppm with allowance up to 100 ppm to absorb single-1-loop formula rounding.

description: Bare Misra-Sudarshan extension on the tubulin Trp radical-pair channel: tau_eff = tau_Z^2 / tau_meas with tau_Z = 1/Delta_ST in cyclic-frequency convention (DeltaE/h; NOT T_2^2/tau_meas backdoor). The bare extension reaches ~110 ns at canonical biological parameters (Delta_ST ~ 30 MHz cyclic, tau_meas ~ 10 ns), about five orders below the 10 ms action-coincidence target and about three orders below the operative O.23 closure-path-(b) ~100 us-1 ms Selection-relevant scale. Expected verdict is BARE_MS_EXTENSION_INSUFFICIENT pending Open Problem O.23 closure (chiral phonon-polariton DFS three-channel + sqrt(N) collective dressing). The verifier treats this verdict as the stable non-pass state; drift to another verdict is the failure mode.

description: Protocol D Drosophila 17O-LORR isotope-shift assay end-to-end Monte Carlo systematic-error propagation through 4-parameter logistic Hill-equation fit (V3 Ch16 sec 16.3.4). Pre-registered structural verdict: the registered S1-S6 systematic budget renders the assay operationally unfalsifiable at Bonferroni-corrected significance across the predicted signed [-0.20 percent, -0.10 percent] effect-size band; closure paths C1/C2/C3 documented at the chapter level.
disposition_text: Protocol D Drosophila 17O-LORR isotope-shift assay end-to-end Monte Carlo systematic-error propagation through 4-parameter logistic Hill-equation fit (V3 Ch16 sec 16.3.4). Pre-registered structural verdict: the registered S1-S6 systematic budget renders the assay operationally unfalsifiable at Bonferroni-corrected significance across the predicted signed [-0.20 percent, -0.10 percent] effect-size band; closure paths C1/C2/C3 documented at the chapter level.
monograph_location: V3 Ch16 sec 16.3.4; App H Open Problem O.21
derivation_status: MC pre-registered as expected-non-pass; the operationally-unfalsifiable verdict is the load-bearing structural finding, not a failure of the assay design
residual_interpretation: BELOW_TARGET_POWER_AT_010_PCT_EFFECT_UNDER_BONFERRONI_CORRECTION means the sec 16.3.4 systematic-error budget as currently registered cannot reject H_0 (no isotope shift) even when the true effect lies in the canonical signed [-0.20 percent, -0.10 percent] band; closure requires C1 (tighten dominant systematics), C2 (declare a wider exploratory band), or C3 (raise N_MC_TRIALS plus expand budget items) before unblinding.
